questions.prompt: When elevation expires before the underlying permit policy does, the application can keep running, just not as administrator.
questions.explanation: ThreatLocker creates two policies in this case: one with elevation enabled until the elevation expires, and one without elevation that continues to permit the app. The app keeps working as a standard user.

questions.prompt: A bookkeeper needs admin to install a quarterly QuickBooks update. Which combination is the most defensible default?
choices: Permanent local-admin on the machine, no ringfence
Per-app elevation with a 4-24h expiration, plus ringfence denying PowerShell / cmd spawn
Force the app to run as standard user
questions.explanation: Time-bounded elevation plus a ringfence is the right default. Permanent local-admin removes the bound. Silent + no-expiration removes the user-awareness and the bound. Forcing standard-user defeats the install.
